# deploymentClass probe: the install's first emitted fact (own-repo / tenant-repo / cloud-tenant)

## Context
Tree line **C1** (#14564 map). The graduated OQ4 contract: `deploymentClass` is the probe's FIRST emitted fact â€” it scopes every subsequent self-configuration decision (tenant/cloud classes are propose-only at EVERY tier; the T1â†’T2 shift rule keys on it).

## The Problem
Every detect-and-propose leaf (T-D) and the tier shift rule need the class BEFORE they act. Detection must be deterministic and honest: own-repo (the canonical checkout), tenant-repo (guest substrate â€” the trust boundary), cloud-tenant (isolation-by-default class), with an explicit `undetermined` that fails toward the most restrictive class (propose-only).

## The Architectural Reality
Pure detector over observable facts (repo remote topology, config presence/ancestry, env markers documented in the DR) â€” no network calls, no credential reads. Emits `{deploymentClass, evidence[], confidence}` â€” evidence-carrying per the #14430 discipline (a class that can't name its evidence is `undetermined`). Placement: ai/ install path (structural-pre-flight at claim; sibling: agent-preflight probes).

## The Fix
One PR: the detector + the evidence schema + fixtures for all three classes + undetermined-fails-restrictive + unit specs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Three classes + undetermined detected from fixtures; evidence lists populated.
- [ ] Undetermined â‡’ most-restrictive behavior contract (spec).
- [ ] Zero network/credential access in the detector (review-checkable).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Acting on the class (T-D leaves) Â· the tier table itself (DR #14671).

## Related
Parent #14564 (line C1) Â· consumes DR (#14671) definitions Â· consumed by all T-D + the shift rule. Sweeps: 03:47Z clean.

Evidence checked:

- Authority state: #14673 consumes the #14671 Decision Record definitions. That authority is currently pending as PR #14837, not merged into `dev`.
- Source/KB sweep: no existing implementation surface or contract-ledger authority for a `deploymentClass` detector schema was found outside this ticket family / pending Decision Record.

`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` Verdict: the detector goal is roadmap-valid, but #14673 is not code-ready as written. Implementing it now would force the claimer to freeze enum names, evidence row shape, confidence semantics, and the `undetermined` fallback before the #14671 authority lands and without a Contract Ledger for downstream consumers.

Required before implementation pickup:
1. Add a Contract Ledger matrix covering the detector function/surface, exact output DTO, allowed `deploymentClass` values, evidence row shape, confidence semantics, `undetermined` fallback, docs/JSDoc obligations, and fixture/unit evidence.
2. Clarify merge order against #14671 / PR #14837: either wait for the Decision Record to land, or explicitly state the temporary authority #14673 may use before that merge.
3. Name the downstream consumers (#14676, T-D leaves, tier-shift rule) in the matrix so review can audit that the shipped schema matches the consumed contract.

Applying `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` as the available label vocabulary for this contract-alignment state.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-05T09:17:12Z

## Contract Ledger proposal â€” deploymentClass first fact

This is a reviewer-authored backfill proposal for the missing contract noted in the intake comment. The implementation should wait for the #14671 Decision Record authority to land, or explicitly cite the temporary authority it is using before that merge.

### Proposed merge-order contract

#14673 should stay `not-code-ready` until #14671 / PR #14837 is merged or the ticket owner posts an explicit temporary authority. The detector output is consumed by #14676 and the later detect-and-propose leaves, so enum spelling and fallback semantics must be stable before implementation.

### Proposed Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Detector function / command | #14564 C1 + #14671 Decision Record | Pure local probe returns deployment classification without network calls or credential reads | Probe failure returns `undetermined`, not a guessed class | Function JSDoc / command help | Unit fixtures for all classes plus failure |
| Output DTO | #14673 consumer contract | `{schemaVersion, deploymentClass, evidence, confidence, checkedAt}` with deterministic enum values | Missing/invalid fields rejected by consumer tests | Schema/JSDoc | DTO validation tests |
| Allowed `deploymentClass` values | #14671 DR | `own-repo`, `tenant-repo`, `cloud-tenant`, `undetermined` unless #14671 chooses different names before merge | Unknown value => fail toward restrictive/propose-only behavior | Decision Record + JSDoc | Fixture per value |
| Evidence row shape | #14430 falsifiability discipline + #14564 C1 | Evidence entries name the observable fact and local source, with no secrets or raw credential values | No evidence => `undetermined` | JSDoc | Tests assert populated evidence for positive classes |
| Restrictive fallback | #14564 C1 | `undetermined` maps to the most restrictive / propose-only behavior for downstream consumers | Consumers must not auto-act on unknown substrate | Consumer note in #14676 and later T-D leaves | Unit test for undetermined fallback |

### Code-ready condition

Remove `not-code-ready` only after #14671 lands or the ticket body/comment names the temporary authority for enum spelling, confidence semantics, and fallback behavior.

ADR-0019 check: detector config, if any, must be an AiConfig leaf read at use site or a pure pre-bootstrap default. No hidden env defaults, no pass-through config bag, no singleton mutation in tests.
